data.body:
PASS 1 review (T3 Integration feasibility — git CLI plumbing). Re-verified live against src/rebar/llm/workflow/snapshot.py + the fetch/rev-parse call sites; confirms and consolidates the prior T3 pass.

AFFIRMATIONS (T3 satisfied):
- Achievable as described: the plan integrates real git plumbing — `git fetch origin`, `rev-parse`-style ref resolution, `read-tree`, `checkout-index`. `git fetch origin` (src/rebar/_engine_support/reads.py:22, .github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml:102) and `git rev-parse ... ^{commit}` (snapshot.py:84) are already used in-repo — MATCH / already-used.
- Auth/HTTPS precondition stated: AC "Private-repo fetch failures surface a descriptive, actionable credential error" names the auth precondition for `git fetch origin`.
- Portability preconditions stated ("pure-Python tar-free extraction, gettempdir/mkdtemp, flock+mkdir fallback, POSIX"); EFS/NFS flock caveat scoped out to alto-fruit-punch.
- Spike-routing present: the janitor primitive was corrected by a spike (dropped PID lease for kernel guarantees) — capability gaps route to a spike.

FINDINGS:

- [ ] Ground the "faithful (NOT lossy `git archive`) materialization via `read-tree` + `checkout-index`" claim against the SPECIFIC content gaps it must close, or route the faithfulness comparison to a spike before committing. [criteria: T3] [location: plan "## What" — "Materialization is faithful (read-tree + checkout-index, NOT lossy git archive)"] Evidence: the existing `git archive` path it replaces (src/rebar/llm/workflow/snapshot.py:23-27) precisely documents what archive drops — `.gitattributes export-ignore` paths, submodule contents (stops at the gitlink), and Git-LFS files (pointer text, not smudged). The plan asserts the new primitive is "faithful" but does not name WHICH gaps it closes nor verify `checkout-index` doesn't SHARE them: `checkout-index` populates from the index and likewise does not recurse into submodule gitlinks, and whether it smudges LFS / honors export-ignore depends on index population + configured filters. This is the load-bearing capability the whole "faithful" success criterion rests on (T3: asserted-but-unverified external capability = MAJOR). suggested_fix: empty — either enumerate the gap(s) closed and cite the plumbing that closes them, or add a spike that diffs an `git archive` tree vs a `read-tree`+`checkout-index` tree on a repo exercising submodules/LFS/export-ignore.

- [ ] State the index/work-tree precondition that makes the `read-tree` + `checkout-index` invocation "lock-free" / free of "git-lock contention," since these plumbing commands default to the repo's single `.git/index` (an index.lock contention point). [criteria: T3] [location: plan AC "Concurrent gates ... run without git-lock contention"; "## What" — "lock-free materialization"] Evidence: AC-4 requires concurrent gates "without git-lock contention" and the success criteria say "lock-free," but `read-tree`/`checkout-index` operate on `.git/index` by default; the lock-free property depends on an unstated index-isolation mechanism (e.g. a per-op `GIT_INDEX_FILE` in a tempdir, or `--prefix` checkout against an ephemeral index). This integration detail determines whether the concurrency AC is technically feasible as described. suggested_fix: empty.
